missables: Point of no return — the final Save Sphere sits at the top of the City of Dying Dreams ramp, just before the Tower of Death. After you enter the Tower and collect 10 crystal items, you cannot return to the airship. Complete all side quests before using that Save Sphere.

City of Dying Dreams glyph doors — kill 10, then more, then 15 fiends in the immediate area to open three sequential doors. Reward: 1 Lv. 4 Key Sphere. If you bypass the glyph without killing the required fiends, the door stays shut permanently.
Tower of Death crystals — small colored crystals appear and fade quickly as you navigate the Tower. Run to touch them before they vanish; you cannot loop back for missed ones.
sgTip: When fighting Behemoth King, use Protect (not Shell) before the killing blow — Protect halves its death Meteor. Alternatively, let an aeon absorb the final hit. In the Jecht fight, put Zombie on him early so the Yu Pagodas damage him with every healing attempt; reapply as it wears off. You cannot carry items or gil into a new game, so spend everything — Spare Change (100,000 gil = 9,999 damage) and aeon Overdrives are free damage.

subLocations.name: Sea of Sorrow
subLocations.prose: After breaking through Sin's exterior, the party enters the Sea of Sorrow. The area map only shows your immediate surroundings, so use the guide map to orient yourself. Treasure chests sit at the tops of waterfalls — you can ride waterfalls down but cannot climb them, so reaching each chest requires a circuitous route upward. Enemies here are extremely powerful and yield heavy AP. The most dangerous is the Behemoth King: when killed, it casts Meteor on the entire party for roughly 6,000 damage to all. Counter this by casting Protect before the killing blow, letting an aeon take the final hit, or simply fleeing. Energy Dragons are vulnerable to Bio — inflict it early for sustained poison damage.

items.name: 20,000 Gil
items.notes: Chest in the Sea of Sorrow

items.name: Lv. 4 Key Sphere
items.notes: Chest at the top of the western falls

items.name: HP Sphere
items.notes: Chest in the northwestern alcove

subLocations.name: City of Dying Dreams
subLocations.prose: Past the Sea of Sorrow's Save Sphere, search the right-hand wall for a glowing glyph. Kill 10 fiends in random encounters to open the first door; a second door requires more kills; a third demands 15. Total: 35 kills for a single Lv. 4 Key Sphere. Watch for Demonolith enemies — their Breath attack can petrify the entire party for an instant game over. Keep at least one front-liner in Stoneproof armor, or fight Demonoliths exclusively with summoned aeons. Treasure is scattered across lift platforms and a spiral slide building: step on the Yevon-symbol platform to ride up to Four-on-One, then cross the lowered bridge for the Defending Bracer. Laevatein is at the bottom of the spiral in the small southern building — press the wall prompt after collecting it to climb back out.

items.name: Lv. 4 Key Sphere
items.notes: Reward for killing 35 fiends (10 + 10 + 15) to open the three glyph doors. All kills must occur in this area.

items.name: Four-on-One (Wakka's weapon)
items.notes: Stand on the Yevon-symbol platform — it rises to the chest.

items.name: Defending Bracer
items.notes: Cross from the Four-on-One chest and push the bridge down to reach this armor.

items.name: 20,000 Gil
items.notes: Found at the ramp down near the bridge.

items.name: HP Sphere
items.notes: Step on the platform beside the 20,000 Gil chest to ride up to this item.

items.name: Defense Sphere
items.notes: Second item on the same platform ride — high above the city.

items.name: Laevatein (Yuna's weapon)
items.notes: At the bottom of the long spiral slide in the small southern building. Press the wall prompt next to the chest to climb back out.

subLocations.name: Tower of Death
subLocations.prose: The Tower crashes to the ground as you approach from the north. Inside, icicles shoot upward — a random battle triggers if one strikes you. Stand still when the camera pans to orient yourself; move away immediately if a white glow appears beneath your feet. Scattered around the tower are small colored crystals that appear and fade rapidly — sprint to touch each one before it vanishes to collect the item inside (mostly weapons and rare spheres: Prism Ball, Mage's Staff, Skill Sphere, Infinity, Hrunting, Knight Lance, and more). Collect 10 items to trigger the final encounter. The last Save Sphere in the game sits near the top of the City of Dying Dreams ramp, just before this tower. This is your absolute last chance to teleport back to the airship and complete any remaining side quests.

items.name: Prism Ball
items.notes: Crystal collectible — touch before it fades.

bosses.strategy: Four Mortiphasms orbit Seymour, each displaying an element — the one closest to him sets what he absorbs and casts. To change his elemental alignment, target a Mortiphasm with attacks or spells: this forces the ring to rotate, shifting which element is active. Your goal is to rotate until you expose a clear elemental weakness, then blast with Lulu's opposing magic. Avoid casting the element he currently absorbs or you will heal him. Have Auron apply Armor Break and Mental Break early. If you mix two element types the discs show, he loses a clear weakness but also loses the devastating Multi-Spell follow-up. Non-elemental damage (Holy, Bio, items) is always safe. Mid-fight, Seymour casts Dispel on the party — this signals his next action will be the non-elemental Ultima. Swap in characters with 5,000+ HP or summon an expendable aeon to absorb it. Kimahri's Mighty Guard adds Shell and elemental Nul-shields as a safety net throughout.

Two Yu Pagodas flank Jecht and attempt to heal him every turn — turn this against him by having Auron inflict Zombie Attack on Jecht early. The Pagodas will then damage him with each heal; reapply Zombie regularly as it wears off. During Tidus's turns, the Trigger Command 'Talk to Jecht' appears — use it when his Overdrive gauge is nearly full to drain a large portion of it. If you cannot prevent his Overdrive, summon an aeon to absorb the hit. Second Form activates after the first 60,000 HP are depleted (total 120,000 HP across both forms): use Mighty Guard, Rikku's Mighty G mix, or Auto-Life on key characters before the transition hits. Finish with everything you have — Overdrives, Mega-Potions thrown at the zombified boss (they damage undead), Spare Change (100,000 gil = 9,999 damage), and aeon Overdrives. You cannot carry items or gil to a new game, so spend freely.

Yu Yevon has permanent Auto-Life — every time you kill it, it immediately revives. The game handles this automatically: continue attacking until the Auto-Life cycles are exhausted and the fight concludes. Before reaching Yu Yevon directly you fight each of your aeons in sequence (they can be Zombied per the EPUB hint). This sequence is functionally impossible to lose; it is scripted to end the game.
